room.description: Room/section counter (0=title, 1-7=rooms). Low write count but high semantic importance — room transitions are major progress events.
room.reward_logic: delta > 0 means forward progress (+10), delta < 0 means backtracking (-2)

boss_flag.description: Boss flag (0=normal, 1-8=boss). 12 writers across banks 0,1,7,8,9 — heavily referenced (29 reads). Boss encounters are critical game events. Values 1-8 correspond to: Gargoyle, Spider, Crimson, Ice, Void, Poison, Knight, Angela.
boss_flag.reward_logic: transition 0->N means boss encounter (+5), transition N->0 means boss defeated (+15)

gameplay_active.description: Gameplay active flag (0=menu, 1=gameplay). Used as gate — reward signals only valid when FFC1=1. Weight 0.0 because it gates other rewards, not a reward itself.
gameplay_active.reward_logic: gate: only apply other rewards when value == 1

lives.description: Lives counter. Despite low static refs (6), runtime shows high volatility (29 changes, 10 unique values). Baseline 3 lives, observed up to 23 — likely includes earned lives. Negative reward on decrease.
lives.reward_logic: delta < 0 means death (-5 per life lost)

hp_main.description: Main HP byte. Stage 1 found 5 health-related code patterns involving this address (increment, decrement, compare with DCDC sub-HP). Sub-HP (DCDC) overflows into this. Decrease = damage taken.
hp_main.reward_logic: delta < 0 means HP loss (-3 per unit), delta > 0 means HP gain (+1 per unit)

powerup.description: Powerup state (0=none, 1=spiral, 2=shield, 3=turbo). HIGHEST write count of any known address (45 writers across 12 banks). Heavily managed — many code paths set/clear powerups. Acquiring a powerup is a moderate positive signal.
powerup.reward_logic: transition 0->N means powerup acquired (+2), transition N->0 means powerup lost (-0.5)

form_change.description: Sara form (0=Witch, 1=Dragon). Read-heavy (11 reads vs 5 writes) — many game systems check current form. Form changes are strategic decisions, small positive signal to encourage exploration.

scroll_x.description: SCX hardware register. Tracks horizontal scroll position — proxy for spatial progress through dungeon.

scroll_y.description: SCY hardware register. Tracks vertical scroll position.

stage_flag.description: Stage flag (0=normal, 1=bonus). High ref count (49) — bonus stages are distinct game modes. Useful for conditioning reward interpretation.

gameplay_mode.description: Gameplay mode/phase (values 0-3). Classified as counter_candidate by Stage 1 (INC pattern). Runtime shows 4 unique values cycling rapidly (57 changes in 600 frames). Likely represents game sub-state (e.g., 0=idle, 1=scrolling, 2=combat, 3=transition). Useful for understanding agent context but NOT a reward signal.

countdown_timer.description: Countdown timer. Static at 255 during scanned gameplay (not in timed section). When active, counts down — could be negative reward for running out.

hp_sub.description: Sub-HP byte. Works with DCDD — overflow/underflow cascades to main HP. Track for granular health monitoring.

section_cycle.description: Section cycle counter (0-5). 6 sections per room. Combined with room counter gives fine-grained progress tracking.

section_descriptor.description: Section descriptor from level data table (bank 13). Read-only from game code — loaded from ROM. Used to derive boss_number = (DC04 - 0x30) / 5 + 1.

dc9c_counter.description: NEW DISCOVERY from Stage 2. Timer-classified, 47 changes, range 0-20, 21 unique values. Not found in Stage 1 static analysis (no direct LDH references — likely accessed via HL pointer). High change rate with bounded range (0-20) suggests an entity timer, spawn counter, or animation frame. Needs further investigation to confirm if score-related.

dcac_counter.description: NEW DISCOVERY from Stage 2. Nearly identical profile to DC9C (46 changes, range 0-20, 21 unique values). Same bounded range suggests these are parallel counters — possibly per-entity timers in an entity table (DC9C and DCAC are 16 bytes apart, consistent with entity struct stride). Track for now; promote to reward if confirmed as kill/score counter.

frame_counter_volatile.description: EXCLUDED from rewards. Frame counter — changes every sample (60/60), 60 unique values, range 0-120. Stage 1 gave it confidence 0.1. Too volatile for reward signal; would dominate reward noise. Only tracked for timing/profiling purposes.

confidence_model.description: Confidence scores derived from Stage 1 cross-reference density and Stage 2 runtime behavior validation

scoring_factors.stage1_write_count: More writers = more code paths affect this address = higher game importance
scoring_factors.stage1_read_count: More readers = more systems depend on this value = higher structural importance
scoring_factors.stage2_stability: Addresses that change meaningfully (not every frame) during gameplay = better reward signals
scoring_factors.known_semantics: Manually verified addresses from reverse engineering get confidence 1.0

rankings_by_importance.name: unknown_ff80
rankings_by_importance.note: High refs but unknown purpose — possible DMA or system state

rankings_by_importance.name: boss_flag
rankings_by_importance.note: Critical game event with heavy read usage (29 reads)

rankings_by_importance.name: gameplay_active
rankings_by_importance.note: Gate flag — many systems check if gameplay is active

rankings_by_importance.name: room
rankings_by_importance.note: Low refs but highest semantic value for progress

rankings_by_importance.name: lives
rankings_by_importance.note: Low refs but critical for survival reward

gating_rules.description: Rules for when reward signals are valid
rules: Boss defeat reward (FFBF N->0) only valid when previous FFBF was 1-8
Room progress reward (FFBD delta>0) only valid when FFD0 (stage_flag) == 0 (normal stage)
HP loss penalty only applied when FFBF == 0 (not during boss — bosses have different HP semantics)
Powerup rewards only when FFC1 == 1 (ignore menu state changes)
Frame counter (FFD4) excluded from all reward calculations

notes: Enhanced config combining Stage 1 static analysis (LDH pattern cross-references across 16 ROM banks) with Stage 2 runtime analysis (600-frame gameplay scan at 10-frame intervals). Key findings: (1) FFC0/powerup has the highest cross-reference density (69 total refs, 45 writers across 12 banks) making it the most actively managed game state variable. (2) FFBF/boss_flag is read-heavy (29 reads vs 12 writes) — many systems check boss state. (3) DC9C and DCAC are new discoveries from runtime with identical bounded profiles (range 0-20, ~46 changes) suggesting per-entity timer/counter arrays accessed via HL pointer (invisible to LDH pattern analysis). (4) FFD4 confirmed as frame counter (60/60 changes, all unique) — excluded from rewards. (5) FFCD identified as 4-value gameplay mode cycling rapidly during play. (6) DCDC/DCDD health system confirmed by 5 health-specific code patterns in Stage 1 with increment/decrement/compare sequences.
